title: Namokar Tirth: Overview and Core Philosophy

location.map_hint: Main entrance visible from Mumbai-Agra National Highway NH-3

content: Namokar Tirth is a prominent Jain pilgrimage site and 'Jain Knowledge City' spread across 40 to 47 acres in the Nashik district of Maharashtra.
The foundation and land worship ceremony for the Tirth took place on February 8, 2014.
The entire site is envisioned as a physical representation of the Panch Parmeshti (Arihant, Siddha, Acharya, Upadhyaya, and Sadhu Parmeshti), aiming to embody their spiritual properties.
The core philosophy is based on the Ratnatraya (the three jewels of Jainism: Right Faith, Right Knowledge, and Right Conduct).
It fosters a 'golden triangle' of Faith, Discipline, and Care for all living beings.
The Tirth serves as a research center for studying the vibrations, scientific effects, and spiritual salvation power of the Namokar Mantra.
rules: Visitors must follow traditional Jain ethical codes of conduct.
Photography may be restricted in specific inner spiritual zones such as the meditation halls.
facilities: Information and registration desk at the entry
Free rickshaw services provided for the elderly, women, and children to navigate the large 47-acre campus
Spiritual guidance center
suggested_questions: What is the philosophy behind Namokar Tirth?
When was the foundation laid?
What is the 'golden triangle'?

title: Geographical Location, Accessibility, and Timings

location.map_hint: Located at Malsane on the Nashik-Dhule Road

content: The official address is Namokar Tirth, Mumbai-Agra National Highway (NH-3), Malsane, Tal. Chandwad, Dist. Nashik, Maharashtra 423101.
The site is located approximately 52 km from Nashik city and is positioned directly on the highway for easy access.
The nearest major railway station is Manmad Junction (27-37 km away), and the closest airport is Ozar Airport in Nashik (42 km away).
The Tirth campus is officially open 24 hours a day for pilgrims, though specific facilities within the campus have designated operational hours.
The official contact number for inquiries is +91-9404006108.
rules: Vehicles must be parked only in the designated visitor parking area to maintain campus serenity.
facilities: Wheelchair accessible entrances and exits
Extensive parking for buses and private vehicles
Highway access and waiting areas for external taxis and buses
suggested_questions: Where is Namokar Tirth located?
Are there wheelchair facilities?
What are the visiting hours?

title: Founder Profile: Acharya Shree 108 Devnandiji Gurudev
content: The Tirth was conceptualized from the deep contemplation of the Maha Mantra by Param Pujya Pragyashraman Srvodayee National Saint Saraswatacharya Shree 108 Devnandiji Gurudev.
Gurudev was born as Mulayamsing in Shahrarh, Madhya Pradesh, and took his Muni Diksha on March 31, 1982, in Hasan, Karnataka.
He is a prolific scholar, having authored more than 40 books, including renowned texts like 'Vastu Chintamani' and 'Dev Shilpa'.
His vision integrates ancient ontological Jain principles with modern social requirements, focusing on non-violence and humanitarian service.
rules: Respectful silence is required when approaching ascetic residential zones (Tyagi Niwas).
facilities: Library containing Gurudev's extensive authored books
Tyagi Niwas for ascetics and visiting monks
suggested_questions: Who founded Namokar Tirth?
What books has Acharya Devnandiji written?
When did Gurudev take Muni Diksha?

title: Samavsharan, Guru Mandir, and Aradhana Center

location.map_hint: Central massive structures visible from the highway

content: The main complex houses a spectacular temple featuring a 32-foot high statue of Bhagwan Chandraprabhu in the meditative Padmasana posture on the first floor.
The site includes a designated 'Guru Mandir' dedicated to honoring great historical Jain gurus and spiritual leaders.
The 'Bolta Samavsharan' is a technological marvel—a massive temple replicating the divine preaching hall, using animation, music, and lighting to explain Jain principles.
The Aradhana Center houses monumental stone idols of the Pancha-Parameṣṭhi, including a majestic 46-foot tall Arihant Parmeshti idol and 36-foot tall Siddha and Acharya idols.
rules: Footwear and all leather items must be removed and stored before entry.
Total silence must be maintained for meditation practitioners in the Aradhana center.
No food items or drinks are allowed inside any of the temple sanctuaries.
facilities: Audio-visual presentation systems in the Samavsharan
Meditation halls
Shoe and bag locker rooms
suggested_questions: How tall is the Bhagwan Chandraprabhu statue?
What is the Bolta Samavsharan?
Are there idols for the Panch Parmeshti?

title: Major Events and Panchkalyanak Mahotsav

location.map_hint: Temporary infrastructures and the 22,000 sq ft hall

content: The Tirth hosts monumental spiritual gatherings, highlighted by the International Panchkalyanak Pratishthan Mahotsav scheduled from February 6 to 25, 2026.
This specific 20-day festival was designed to attract over 1.2 million devotees globally and brought together over 400 Digambar Jain monks.
During such festivals, approximately 3,000 newly crafted idols undergo Pran Pratishtha (the sacred consecration ceremony).
For large events, a massive 22,000 square foot pillarless hall is utilized to accommodate the massive congregations.
rules: Early registration is mandatory for pilgrims seeking overnight stay or meals during major festivals.
Strict adherence to crowd control and event security protocols is required.
facilities: 22,000 sq ft pillarless congregation hall
Temporary medical units and ambulances (during festivals)
450 temporary toilet blocks for sanitation (during festivals)

How many monks attend the festival?

title: Accommodation, Dining, and Future Infrastructure

location.map_hint: Behind the main temple complex and borders of the campus

content: The Tirth provides extensive accommodation via a Deluxe Dharamshala containing over 200 rooms, including AC, Non-AC, and suite options with hot water, lifts, and backup generators.
A Bhojanshala (dining hall) operates under strict Jain sunset rules (no food after sunset, no root vegetables), providing breakfast, lunch, and dinner to pilgrims.
A massive State Government approved development plan worth Rs 36.35 crore is currently active to add permanent concrete roads and advanced sanitation.
Future luxury implementations under construction include a helipad for VIP/Emergency access, boating facilities, and an extremely well-equipped modern tourist canteen.
rules: Check-in and check-out time for rooms is standard 12:00 PM.
Consumption of food after sunset is strictly prohibited by Tirth policy.
Only Jain and vegetarian Hindu pilgrims are permitted to stay in the residential blocks.
facilities: Deluxe Dharmashala (over 200 rooms)
Pure vegetarian Bhojanshala with filtered RO water
Future/Upcoming: Boating facilities and Helipad
Event halls and banquet spaces with in-house catering (for Dharmic events)
suggested_questions: Does Namokar Tirth have rooms for stay?
Are there dining facilities available?
Is there a helipad on the campus?

title: Knowledge City, Medical, and Animal Services

location.map_hint: North and Edge sides of the campus

content: As a designated 'Jain Knowledge City', future expansions include a Jain Gurukul, secular schools, colleges, ITIs, and specialized women's training centers.
The Tirth currently operates an Ayurvedic Hospital providing traditional healthcare, and dispatches Mobile Hospital Units to serve rural and tribal populations nearby.
Embodying the Jain principle of Ahimsa (non-violence), the Tirth provides veterinary care clinics and animal shelters for the welfare of local wildlife and livestock.
There is also a Prayashchit Mandir (Addiction Salvation Temple) acting as a spiritual hospital for those seeking psychological well-being and freedom from addictions.
rules: Treat all animals on the campus with strict compassion; harming animals is severely punished.
Maintain the privacy of seekers utilizing the Prayashchit Mandir counseling.
facilities: Ayurvedic Hospital
Mobile Ambulance Units
Veterinary clinic and shelter
Jinvani Upasana Kendra (Library and Literature Study Center)
suggested_questions: Is there a hospital at the Tirth?
What education facilities are planned?
Does the Tirth treat animals?

title: Mandatory Code of Conduct and Restrictions

location.map_hint: Signages at main gate and temple entrances

content: The entire Namokar Tirth operates on strict Jain principles to maintain its spiritual purity.
Leather items of any kind (such as shoes, belts, wallets, and bags) are strictly forbidden inside the temple and main spiritual premises.
Modest, respectful clothing is required for all visitors (shoulders and knees must be completely covered).
Consumption of alcohol, tobacco products (smoking/chewing), and any non-vegetarian food is strictly and permanently prohibited on the 47-acre campus.
rules: Zero tolerance for leather goods inside temples.
Zero tolerance for smoking, alcohol, or non-vegetarian items.
Mobile phones should be switched off or put on silent mode inside all spiritual complexes.
facilities: Secure locker rooms strictly for storing leather items and electronics
Dedicated shoe stands outside all temples

content: I am specifically designed to function as 'Nimittam', the spiritual guide providing detailed information about the Namokar Tirth in Nashik.

For inquiries unrelated to this pilgrimage site Jain philosophy, or the facilities herein, please refer to general resources, though I can answer general knowledge questions using my base capabilities.
rules: Politely deflect queries completely unrelated to the Tirth unless they fall under general acceptable chat.
